hierarchical.message:
Hierarchical view — comparing SAL-ORD-2026-00054-BLD-0225-SNAP-0384 (previous build) against SAL-ORD-2026-00054-BLD-0225-SNAP-0386 (this build). Context: Changes only.
This is a comparison of two configured builds. It exists so that knowledge from the previous build can be amended, not blindly reused — only the lines below are different between the two builds. Everything not shown is unchanged and can be built the same way as before.
AddedRemovedRevision changedQty changedMoved
What do these mean? (click to expand)
➕ AddedThis node is new in this build — it did not exist at this position in the previous build's tree.
➖ RemovedThis node was in the previous build at this position and is gone in this one.
✏ ChangedThis node exists in both builds at the same position, but its quantity and/or its BOM revision changed. The What-changed column spells out which.
Indentation / LevelRows are indented by their depth in the assembly. A change shown under a parent assembly means that change happens inside that assembly. Parent rows are kept (even when unchanged) so you can see where in the structure each change lives.
Revision changedThe part is sourced from a different BOM revision than before. Per the rule we use: a row is flagged only when its own sourcing BOM revision changes. A parent assembly's revision bumping does NOT flag its children unless the child itself actually changed — so if a child is unflagged, its use is genuinely unchanged and prior knowledge still applies.
Switch View Mode for the engineering tree vs. the flat procurement list. Use Download Diff Workbook for a color-coded export you can send to the builder.

flat_procurement.message:
Flat Procurement view — comparing SAL-ORD-2026-00054-BLD-0225-SNAP-0384 (previous build) against SAL-ORD-2026-00054-BLD-0225-SNAP-0386 (this build). Context: Changes only.
This is a comparison of two configured builds. It exists so that knowledge from the previous build can be amended, not blindly reused — only the lines below are different between the two builds. Everything not shown is unchanged and can be built the same way as before.
AddedRemovedRevision changedQty changedMoved
What do these mean? (click to expand)
AddedA part that was NOT on the previous build and IS on this one. You must source/buy it for this build.
RemovedA part that WAS on the previous build and is NOT on this one. Do not buy or include it this time.
Revision changedThe same part, but it is now sourced from a different BOM revision than before. The part number may be identical — what changed is the revision of the bill of materials it comes from. Treat it as: re-verify this part against the new revision.
Qty changedThe same part, but the total quantity needed across the whole build is different. The Total Qty column shows previous → this build.
Revision A → B columnShows the BOM revision(s) the part is sourced from, previous build on the left, this build on the right. If a part is used in more than one BOM, you may see more than one revision listed — that means the part lives in multiple bills of material, each at its own revision. That is normal for shared/common parts.
Switch View Mode for the engineering tree vs. the flat procurement list. Use Download Diff Workbook for a color-coded export you can send to the builder.
